items.title: Fundamentals of DevOps & Software Delivery • Yevgeniy "Jim" Brikman & Kief Morris • GOTO 2025
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. #GOTOcon #GOTObookclub
http://gotopia.tech/bookclub

Check out more here:
https://gotopia.tech/episodes/405

Yevgeniy "Jim" Brikman - Author of "Fundamentals of DevOps and Software Delivery" & Co-Founder of Gruntwork @YevgeniyBrikman 
Kief Morris - Author of "Infrastructure as Code" & Distinguished Engineer at Thoughtworks @KiefMorris 

RESOURCES
Yevgeniy (Jim)
https://bsky.app/profile/brikis98.bsky.social
https://twitter.com/brikis98
https://github.com/brikis98/
https://www.linkedin.com/in/jbrikman
https://www.ybrikman.com

Kief
https://bsky.app/profile/kief.com
https://twitter.com/kief
https://github.com/kief
https://www.linkedin.com/in/kiefmorris
https://infrastructure-as-code.com
https://kief.com

Links
https://terragrunt.gruntwork.io
https://youtu.be/OvspPZlilkQ
https://youtu.be/dMwGfRINpz0
https://youtu.be/MCPrtYxuVPU
https://youtu.be/MYVrLXKJp0Y
https://youtu.be/gWPPnuQB4co
https://youtu.be/XhuiU0Kjvf0
https://youtu.be/IW-cPuydd5s
https://youtu.be/zt9DFMkjkEA
https://youtu.be/Th4MFWJmlsc
https://youtu.be/grEueqT24YI
https://youtu.be/vvFdTp54gII
https://youtu.be/Wp8huqq_qgI
https://youtu.be/1f_P8ZH5BC8
https://youtu.be/1PJM8p-RMsY
https://youtu.be/K843Ukiw3d8
https://youtu.be/5AGEv0sAw6g
https://youtu.be/SfIvrtDsE3Q
https://youtu.be/zmA5fhV-FGk

DESCRIPTION
Yevgeniy (Jim) Brikman, author of "Fundamentals of DevOps and Software Delivery", discusses his journey from app developer to DevOps advocate, triggered by LinkedIn's deployment crisis that required freezing all product development for months. The discussion with Kief Morris explores the practical definition of DevOps as efficient software delivery methodology, the relationship between infrastructure as code and application orchestration tools, the necessity of frameworks over custom wrapper scripts, and emerging paradigms including infrastructure from code, infrastructure as graph models, and interactive runbooks.

Jim emphasizes that while new approaches are interesting, maturity and standardization in existing tools often provides more value than constantly chasing new technologies.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
01:10 The LinkedIn transformation
04:29 Defining DevOps
06:43 Platform engineering & the infrastructure stack
20:21 The framework question
28:17 The future of infrastructure as code
36:52 Outro

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Yevgeniy Brikman • Fundamentals of DevOps and Software Delivery • https://amzn.to/3WMPMFU
Yevgeniy Brikman • Terraform: Up and Running • https://amzn.to/4otpxQL

Mauricio Salatino • Platform Engineering on Kubernetes • https://amzn.to/3X14qZK

items.title: Embracing Complexity in Serverless • Sheen Brisals • GOTO 2025
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This presentation was recorded at GOTO Serverless 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOserverless
https://conferences.gotopia.tech/goto-serverless-bengaluru-2025

Sheen Brisals - AWS Serverless Hero, Engineering Leader, O'Reilly Author @sheenbrisals6427 

RESOURCES
https://twitter.com/sheenbrisals
https://www.linkedin.com/in/sheen-brisals
https://sbrisals.medium.com
https://www.sheenbrisals.com

ABSTRACT
As humans, we manage the most complex system in the world: our bodies. As engineers and technicians, we have been building and operating some of the most complex systems on Earth and beyond for centuries. However, when we encounter modern cloud applications that serve us in our daily lives, we often find ourselves complaining that they are complicated. We label the communication lines in distributed systems as complex.

Why is that? Are we losing our ingenuity in dealing with modern applications?

This talk will explore the complexities of modern systems and help you understand the various types of complexity, both beneficial and detrimental. It will also provide strategies for effectively managing complexity while building cloud and serverless applications. By learning how to embrace complexity, you will be better equipped to grow your business beyond barriers! [...]

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
04:05 Agenda
04:58 Acknowledging complexity
08:47 Understanding complexity
12:58 Managing complexity
26:22 Closing thoughts
26:54 Outro

Download slides and read the full abstract here:
https://conferences.gotopia.tech/goto-serverless-bengaluru-2025/sessions/3843

items.title: Platform Engineering: From Theory to Practice • Liz Fong-Jones & Lesley Cordero
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted.
http://gotopia.tech

Read the full transcription of this interview here:
https://gotopia.tech/articles/384

Liz Fong-Jones - Field CTO at Honeycomb.io
Lesley Cordero - Staff Software Engineer, Tech Lead at The New York Times

RESOURCES
Liz
https://bsky.app/profile/lizthegrey.com
https://github.com/lizthegrey
https://linkedin.com/in/efong
https://www.lizthegrey.com

Lesley
https://www.lesleycordero.com
https://twitter.com/clesleycode
https://github.com/clesleycode
https://www.linkedin.com/in/lesleycordero

Videos
https://www.alex-hidalgo.com
https://www.honeycomb.io/blog/most-important-developer-productivity-metric-build-times

DESCRIPTION
Liz and Lesley explore the evolution of platform engineering from its DevOps and SRE roots. They discuss the challenges of building effective developer platforms, the importance of psychological safety and evidence-based prioritization, the complexities of open source sustainability, and the delicate balance between centralized platform teams and developer autonomy.

The conversation covers practical insights on documentation automation, onboarding strategies, the manager-engineer career pendulum, and why treating platform work as a service rather than a mandate is crucial for organizational success.

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Adkins, Beyer, Blankinship, Lewandowski, Oprea & Stubblefield • Building Secure and Reliable Systems • https://amzn.to/4n0bjae
Charity Majors, Liz Fong-Jones & George Miranda • Observability Engineering • https://amzn.to/38scbma
Beyer, Murphy, Rensin, Kawahara & Thorne • The Site Reliability Workbook • https://amzn.to/3IwsiOl
Kelly Shortridge & Aaron Rinehart • Security Chaos Engineering • https://www.verica.io/sce-book
No

items.title: What's New in Rust 1.81-1.?? Rustacean Station marathon
items.author: Jon Gjengset
items.content:
<article>Ben and Jon have yet again, and still completely intentionally, fallen behind on the "What's New in Rust" series from Rustacean Station (https://rustacean-station.org/) where they discuss each new Rust release. And all so that they (we?) could do another live marathon stream to go through what's changed across a bunch of Rust releases together!

You can listen to the last What's New in Rust podcast episode (covering 1.79 and 1.80) at https://rustacean-station.org/episode/rust-1.79-1.80/ or wherever you listen to podcasts.

The recording of this episode will be posted to the regular Rustacean Station feed once it's been cut appropriately :)</article>

items.title: Holistic Engineering: Organic Problem Solving • Vanessa Formicola & Andrew Harmel-Law • GOTO 2025
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted. #GOTOcon #GOTOunscripted
https://gotopia.tech

Check out more here:
https://gotopia.tech/articles/403

Vanessa Formicola - Principal Architect & Engineer at Prima
Andrew Harmel-Law - Technical Principal at Thoughtworks & Author of "Facilitating Software Architecture" @AndrewHarmelLaw 

RESOURCES
Vanessa
https://bsky.app/profile/vanessaformicola.bsky.social
https://x.com/V_Formicola
https://www.linkedin.com/in/vanessa-formicola

Andrew
https://bsky.app/profile/andrewhl.bsky.social
https://twit.social/@ahl
https://x.com/al94781
https://github.com/andrewharmellaw
https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewharmellaw
https://andrewharmellaw.github.io

Links
https://facilitatingsoftwarearchitecture.com
https://youtu.be/ZQyd4rVfl4w
https://youtu.be/zx1C_MKncv0
https://youtu.be/_9vi70yFBDg
https://youtu.be/HKS5sKsIkkc
https://youtu.be/H0EK0B1QvVA
https://youtu.be/Saaz6D1azlU
https://youtu.be/zl5U8KO28rM
https://youtu.be/PK-y-iG5VnE
https://youtu.be/NGXl1D-KwRI
https://youtu.be/YpFR8qzwYSA
https://youtu.be/hDuVtEM0iKg
https://youtu.be/ZPlD0rpiFwQ
https://youtu.be/xtmc-gTSPeA

DESCRIPTION
Principal Software Engineer Vanessa Formicola introduces the concept of "holistic engineering" – the practice of making technical decisions while factoring in non-technical forces that inevitably impact code and architecture. Through patterns like the "solo coding event", "everything but the kitchen sink" and "space abuse" she demonstrates how human behaviors, organizational dynamics, career frameworks, and company processes leave traces in codebases that no one intentionally designed.

Vanessa argues that technical leaders must look beyond pure technical excellence to understand the organic system they're working within, advocating for honest conversations about constraints and realistic architectural decisions that account for human factors.
The discussion extends to the emerging need for ethical engineering as our technical decisions increasingly impact society at unprecedented speed, with practical advice for teams to analyze their own systems through the lens of tech, people, and product dimensions.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
01:19 What is Holistic Engineering?
06:28 Patterns in the wild
09:31 The domain library problem
11:58 The gap between theory & practice
13:34 Taking a broader view: The challenge of organizational forces
15:17 Recognizing the slippery slope
18:13 Strategies for recognition & mitigation
26:55 Starting small: Team-level analysis
33:51 The future: Ethical engineering & changing technology
36:50 Code as a mirror
38:11 Outro

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Andrew Harmel-Law • Facilitating Software Architecture • https://amzn.eu/d/5kZKVfU
Adam Tornhill • Your Code as a Crime Scene • https://amzn.to/3FI5E2V
Christopher Alexander • A Pattern Language • https://amzn.to/3Urefj3
Diana Montalion • Learning Systems Thinking • https://amzn.to/3ZpycdJ
Donella H. Meadows • Thinking in Systems • https://amzn.to/3XtqYCV
Adrienne Braganza Tacke • "Looks Good to Me": Constructive Code Reviews • https://amzn.to/3E75XrD
Stefan Hofer & Henning Schwentner • Domain Storytelling • https://amzn.to/3EroBH7
Vlad Khononov • Balancing Coupling in Software Design • https://amzn.to/4iGpbDv
Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais • Team Topologies • http://amzn.to/3sVLyLQ
